UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user of the FinTrack Automation application. This entity stores user-specific settings and identifies ownership of organizations, but does not store authentication credentials.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

firstName.description: The first name of the user.

lastName.description: The last name of the user.

email.description: The email address of the user, used for communication.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

Organization.title: Organization
Organization.description: Represents a business or personal entity whose financial data is being managed within the application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Organization entity.

name.description: The legal or common name of the organization.

description.description: A brief description of the organization or its purpose.

ownerUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile that owns this organization. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Organization)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the organization record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the organization record was last updated.

FinancialAccount.title: FinancialAccount
FinancialAccount.description: Represents a financial account (e.g., bank account, credit card, cash account) belonging to an organization.

id.description: Unique identifier for the FinancialAccount entity.

name.description: A display name for the financial account (e.g., 'Business Checking', 'Credit Card').

accountType.description: The type of the financial account (e.g., 'Bank', 'Credit Card', 'Cash', 'Investment').

currency.description: The currency code for the account (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR').

organizationId.description: Reference to the Organization this financial account belongs to. (Relationship: Organization 1:N FinancialAccount)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the financial account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the financial account was last updated.

Category.title: Category
Category.description: Represents a classification for financial transactions (e.g., 'Rent', 'Utilities', 'Salary').

id.description: Unique identifier for the Category entity.

name.description: The name of the category (e.g., 'Office Supplies', 'Travel').

type.description: The type of category, indicating if it's for income or expense (e.g., 'Income', 'Expense').

organizationId.description: Reference to the Organization this category belongs to, allowing for organization-specific categories. (Relationship: Organization 1:N Category)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the category was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the category was last updated.

Contact.title: Contact
Contact.description: Represents a person or business an organization interacts with, such as customers or vendors.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Contact entity.

name.description: The name of the contact (e.g., 'Acme Corp', 'John Doe').

email.description: The email address of the contact.

phone.description: The phone number of the contact.

address.description: The postal address of the contact.

organizationId.description: Reference to the Organization this contact is associated with. (Relationship: Organization 1:N Contact)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the contact was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the contact was last updated.

Document.title: Document
Document.description: Represents an uploaded financial document (e.g., invoice PDF, receipt image) from which data can be extracted.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Document entity.

fileName.description: The original file name of the uploaded document.

fileUrl.description: The URL or path to the stored document file.

uploadDate.description: The date and time when the document was uploaded.

organizationId.description: Reference to the Organization that owns this document. (Relationship: Organization 1:N Document)

extractedText.description: The full text extracted from the document using OCR, if available.

status.description: The processing status of the document (e.g., 'Uploaded', 'Processing', 'Processed', 'Error').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the document record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the document record was last updated.

Transaction.title: Transaction
Transaction.description: Represents a single financial income or expense event within an organization.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Transaction entity.

date.description: The date when the transaction occurred.

amount.description: The monetary amount of the transaction. Positive for income, negative for expense.

description.description: A brief description or memo for the transaction.

transactionType.description: Indicates if the transaction is an 'Income' or 'Expense'.

financialAccountId.description: Reference to the FinancialAccount from which the transaction originated or was posted. (Relationship: FinancialAccount 1:N Transaction)

categoryId.description: Reference to the Category assigned to this transaction. (Relationship: Category 1:N Transaction)

contactId.description: Optional reference to a Contact involved in the transaction (e.g., payer, payee). (Relationship: Contact 1:N Transaction)

isAutomated.description: Indicates if the transaction data was automatically extracted and entered by the system (e.g., via OCR).

extractedFromDocumentId.description: Optional reference to the Document from which this transaction's data was extracted. (Relationship: Document 1:N Transaction)

organizationId.description: Reference to the Organization this transaction belongs to. (Relationship: Organization 1:N Transaction)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the transaction record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the transaction record was last updated.

Invoice.title: Invoice
Invoice.description: Represents an invoice issued by an organization to a customer, used for accounts receivable management.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Invoice entity.

issueDate.description: The date when the invoice was issued.

dueDate.description: The date by which the invoice payment is due.

totalAmount.description: The total monetary amount of the invoice.

status.description: The current status of the invoice (e.g., 'Draft', 'Sent', 'Paid', 'Overdue', 'Cancelled').

customerId.description: Reference to the Contact representing the customer this invoice was issued to. (Relationship: Contact 1:N Invoice)

organizationId.description: Reference to the Organization that issued this invoice. (Relationship: Organization 1:N Invoice)

paymentTerms.description: Description of the payment terms (e.g., 'Net 30', 'Due on Receipt').

notes.description: Any additional notes or comments for the invoice.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the invoice record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the invoice record was last updated.

InvoiceLineItem.title: InvoiceLineItem
InvoiceLineItem.description: Represents a single item or service listed on an invoice.

id.description: Unique identifier for the InvoiceLineItem entity.

description.description: A description of the product or service being billed.

quantity.description: The quantity of the item or service.

unitPrice.description: The price per unit of the item or service.

amount.description: The total amount for this line item (quantity * unitPrice).

invoiceId.description: Reference to the Invoice this line item belongs to. (Relationship: Invoice 1:N InvoiceLineItem)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the invoice line item was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the invoice line item was last updated.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. Each document is uniquely identified by the user's Firebase Authentication UID, which corresponds to `{userId}` in the path. Access is restricted to the owning user.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the user.

definition.description: Organizations owned by a specific user. The `{userId}` in the path corresponds to the `ownerUserId` of the organization. Access is restricted to the owning user. Documents will include a denormalized 'ownerUserId' field for explicit authorization checks and query flexibility.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the user who owns this organization.

params.name: organizationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the organization.

definition.description: Financial accounts belonging to a specific organization. Documents include denormalized 'organizationId' and 'ownerUserId' (from the parent organization) to enable direct authorization and efficient queries without `get()` calls to parent documents.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the user who owns the parent organization.

params.name: organizationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent organization.

params.name: financialAccountId
params.description: The unique identifier for the financial account.

definition.description: Transaction categories specific to an organization. Documents include denormalized 'organizationId' and 'ownerUserId' (from the parent organization) for authorization independence and efficient querying.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the user who owns the parent organization.

params.name: organizationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent organization.

params.name: categoryId
params.description: The unique identifier for the category.

definition.description: Customers or vendors associated with an organization. Documents include denormalized 'organizationId' and 'ownerUserId' (from the parent organization) for authorization independence and efficient querying.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the user who owns the parent organization.

params.name: organizationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent organization.

params.name: contactId
params.description: The unique identifier for the contact.

definition.description: Uploaded financial documents for an organization. Documents include denormalized 'organizationId' and 'ownerUserId' (from the parent organization) for authorization independence and efficient querying.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the user who owns the parent organization.

params.name: organizationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent organization.

params.name: documentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the document.

definition.description: Financial transactions recorded for an organization. Documents include denormalized 'organizationId' and 'ownerUserId' (from the parent organization) for authorization independence and efficient querying.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the user who owns the parent organization.

params.name: organizationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent organization.

params.name: transactionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the transaction.

definition.description: Invoices issued by an organization. Documents include denormalized 'organizationId' and 'ownerUserId' (from the parent organization) for authorization independence and efficient querying.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the user who owns the parent organization.

params.name: organizationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent organization.

params.name: invoiceId
params.description: The unique identifier for the invoice.

definition.description: Individual line items for a specific invoice. Documents include denormalized 'invoiceId', 'organizationId', and 'ownerUserId' (from the parent invoice and organization) to maintain authorization independence and query efficiency.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the user who owns the parent organization.

params.name: organizationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent organization.

params.name: invoiceId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent invoice.

params.name: lineItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for the invoice line item.

firestore.reasoning:
The FinTrack Automation Firestore structure prioritizes secure, scalable, and debuggable authorization by adhering to the core design principles of Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and consistent Access Modeling. Each user's data, including their organizations and all related financial entities, is nested under their unique user ID, establishing a clear hierarchy of ownership.

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization (CRITICAL):**
To eliminate the need for costly and complex `get()` calls in Firestore security rules, the `ownerUserId` from the parent `UserProfile` and the `organizationId` from the parent `Organization` are denormalized into all child documents. For instance, a `Transaction` document (path: `/users/{userId}/organizations/{organizationId}/transactions/{transactionId}`) will explicitly contain both `organizationId` and `ownerUserId` fields. This allows security rules to evaluate authorization solely based on the `request.auth.uid` matching `userId` in the path (for user-level collections) or matching the `ownerUserId` field within the document itself (for nested collections), without fetching parent documents. This ensures atomic operations, simplifies rule debugging, and enhances performance.

**QAPs (Queries Are Not Filters) Support:**
Structural segregation is key to supporting Query-Able Paths (QAPs). By nesting organizations under `/users/{userId}/organizations/{organizationId}`, and all subsequent financial data (accounts, categories, contacts, documents, transactions, invoices) as subcollections of organizations, all documents within a given collection share the same security posture. For example, all `financialAccounts` under `/users/{userId}/organizations/{organizationId}/financialAccounts` are owned by the `{userId}` and belong to `{organizationId}`. This allows for efficient `list` operations, as client-side queries can only request data for which the user is already authorized by their UID and the specific organization ID. Security rules can then simply verify that `request.auth.uid` matches the `{userId}` wildcard in the path, or the denormalized `ownerUserId` in the document, ensuring that no unauthorized data is ever returned, making rules simpler and more effective than relying on server-side filters.

**Summary of Structure:**
- User-specific data (`UserProfile`, `Organization`) is housed under `/users/{userId}`.
- Organization-specific data (`FinancialAccount`, `Category`, `Contact`, `Document`, `Transaction`, `Invoice`) are subcollections under `/users/{userId}/organizations/{organizationId}`.
- Further nested data (`InvoiceLineItem`) is a subcollection of `Invoice`.

This design ensures that security rules are concise (e.g., `allow read, write: if request.auth.uid == userId && resource.data.organizationId == organizationId;`), easy to audit, and robust against common authorization pitfalls.
